sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
Gaia Data Release 3
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, radial_velocity)

meta.notes: Mass: Mason, Hartkopf & Miles 2017 §3 (page 6) paper body and abstract both state 0.2041 +/- 0.0064 Msun for the M dwarf companion (mass sum 0.776 +/- 0.024 Msun split via Heintz 1974 mass ratio with the van Leeuwen 2007 Hipparcos parallax). Bond et al. 2017 lists 0.573 Msun for 40 Eri B while Mason 2017 lists 0.575 Msun from the same dynamics — paper-level disagreement at the per-mille level, both within 1 sigma. We adopt Bond 2017 for B (recommended) and Mason 2017 for C (recommended). Mann et al. 2015 also independently reports a semi-empirical M_K-based mass M = 0.222 +/- 0.022 Msun (Table 5 'Gl 166 C' row, observed) and a Dartmouth model-fit mass 0.233 +/- 0.011 Msun (Table 7 same row, inferred). Neither is recorded as a separate entry here; future schema-tier work could add the Table 5 value as recommended:false. Radius / Teff: Mann et al. 2015 reports BOTH an observed Table 5 row and a Dartmouth-inferred Table 7 row for 'Gl 166 C'. Table 5 (Parameters of All Stars in Our Sample, page 29) gives R = 0.274 +/- 0.011 Rsun and Teff = 3167 +/- 61 K (this is the row the DB uses). Table 7 (Inferred stellar evolution model parameters, page 36) gives R = 0.242 +/- 0.004 Rsun and Teff = 3362 +/- 18 K. The structured method fields 'spectroscopic_calibration' and 'low_res_spectroscopy' match the Table 5 derivation (M_K-band radius relation + BT-Settl atmosphere fit), so Table 5 values are the consistent paper actual. A short-lived 2026-05-29 mid-session edit briefly substituted the Table 7 model-fit values; a verification subagent caught the resulting method/value mismatch and the Table 5 values were restored in the immediate follow-up commit. Age: system-coeval with 40 Eri B via Bond 2017 §6.2 IFMR-derived progenitor lifetime ~1.8 Gyr; uncertainty 0.5 Gyr is a curator estimate (paper reports only '~1.8 Gyr'). Mann 2015 Table 7 best-fit age log(age)=9.44 (~2.75 Gyr) with sigma_age=0.60 dex is consistent within 1 sigma. Luminosity: Cifuentes et al. 2020 CARMENES catalog row matched by Karmn J04153-076 / GJ 166 C identifier; the value L = (6.51 +/- 0.13) x 10^-3 Lsun listed above was carried in from the 2026-05-27 working notes and has NOT been independently verified against CDS Table A.3 in the 2026-05-29 pass. Mann 2015 implied L from F_bol = 0.87259 x 10^-8 erg s^-1 cm^-2 (the same Table 5 row) and d = 5.04 pc gives ~6.9 x 10^-3 Lsun (~6% offset); resolution deferred to a follow-up VizieR query. Metallicity: Mann et al. 2015 Table 5 actually DOES report a direct metallicity for Gl 166 C: [Fe/H] = -0.21 +/- 0.08 dex (M_K-band photometric calibration, consistent within 1.5 sigma with the 40 Eri A value of -0.31 +/- 0.03 from Sousa et al. 2008). The earlier 'N/A, inherit from A' decision was a false-negative carried in from the 2026-05-27 working notes; adding the Mann 2015 [Fe/H] entry is deferred to a follow-up session to avoid scope creep beyond the warmup. Rotation: 2026-05-27 working notes flagged Kemmer 2025 P_rot ~ 137 d as provisional and deferred; the 2026-05-29 lit-search pass could not independently locate a Kemmer 2025 paper, while Suarez Mascareno et al. 2024 (CARMENES VII, 2024A&A...684A...9S) and Diez Alonso et al. 2019 (CARMENES IV, 2019A&A...621A.126D) are the more plausible primary photometric rotation sources for this star. Deferred to a follow-up CARMENES-catalog verification. Activity (H-alpha): 40 Eri C is the variable star DY Eri, an optically active flare star; multi-survey H-alpha equivalent-width values scatter over roughly pEW = -2 to -4 Angstrom across CARMENES / Schoefer / Newton samples, no single-paper anchor curated in this pass. Deferred.
